{{神社 |名称=蒲生八幡神社 |画像=[[ファイル:IMG kamou shrine.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地=福岡県北九州市小倉南区蒲生5-6 |位置={{Coord|33|50|13.85|N|130|51|30.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[宗像三女神]]、[[大山祇命]]、[[細川幽斎]]、[[細川忠興]]　 |社格=県社 |創建= |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Wards of Kitakyushu#Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan|label={{nowrap|蒲生八幡神社}}|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} [[ファイル:蒲生八幡神社 第三の鳥居と楼門.jpg|right|thumb|200px|第三の鳥居と楼門]] '''蒲生八幡神社'''（かもうはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉南区]][[蒲生 (北九州市)|蒲生]]（がもう）に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧社格は[[県社]]。 == 御祭神 == *[[応神天皇]] *[[神功皇后]] *[[宗像三女神]] *[[大山祇命]] *[[細川幽斎]] *[[細川忠興]]　 == 歴史 == 往昔、蒲生村虹山（巣山）の峯に[[多紀理比売命]]・[[多岐都比売命]]・[[市寸島比売命]]の三神が天降り、「我、永く此地を守らん」と告げる。そこで、里人は紫の池の辺に多紀理比売命を祀り、虹山の山頂に多岐都比売命を祀り（蒲生宮）、紫の池の北に市寸島比売命を祀った。後に、蒲生宮に[[応神天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]を祀り、蒲生八幡宮とした。 [[元暦]]元年甲辰年に[[壇ノ浦の戦い]]で[[平氏]]が敗戦。[[安徳天皇|安徳帝]]は入水し、[[三種の神器]]も海中に沈む。[[源頼朝]]はこのことに嘆き、規矩郡（[[企救郡]]）高浜浦の漁人の長、岩松与三に神器を探し出すよう命じた。岩松は蒲生八幡宮に神器発見・安全を祈願したのち海に入り、鏡と勾玉を網に得ることができ、源頼朝に献上。岩松はこの功績により、規矩一郡を与えられるが、蒲生の神々の御神徳によるものとし、一郡すべてを神社に寄進した。 [[元弘]]・[[正慶]]・[[建武 (日本)|建武]]年間、虹山城主・規矩氏に崇敬を受け、多くの神領を与えられる。[[応永]]年間には、規矩親忠が神殿を建立。[[大永]]2年4月に小倉城主・小野田種尚より、規矩郡大野庄平田二反を与えられる。 [[天正]]のはじめに、[[大友義鎮|大友宗麟]]の兵火に罹り、神社焼失。このことを憂いた、岩松弥兵衛（三郎兵衛）は高浜（現在の[[井筒屋]]付近）に神社を造立し、社号を規矩（企救）八幡宮（高浜八幡宮）とした。小倉城主・[[高橋鑑種]]が社殿造営し、[[鳥居]]を建立する。天正年間、小倉城主・毛利成儀（勝信）からも篤い崇敬を受ける。[[慶長]]7年、[[小倉城]]築城に伴い、[[細川忠興]]は神社が鎮座する高浜を東[[曲輪]]とするために規矩八幡宮をもとの蒲生の地（中島山）に遷し、社号を再び蒲生八幡宮とした。[[寛永]]9年に入国した[[小笠原氏|小笠原]]藩からも篤い崇敬を受け、社殿造営等がなされた。 [[2011年]]（平成23年）[[12月27日]]、北九州市教育委員会は社殿を北九州市の[[有形文化財]]に指定した[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定]西日本新聞 2011年12月28日。 == 特徴 == *小倉で最古の歴史を有する神社。 *[[中世]]、小倉一円の[[産土神]]となる。（小倉郷土会） *[[氏子]]の多さが県下屈指である。その範囲は近郷のほか、小倉中心街にまでわたる。 *[[厄祓い]]・[[厄除]]の神社として[[信仰]]を集める。 *御社殿建築　御[[幣殿]]は北九州市で最古であり、御[[本殿]]は旧[[豊前国]]で最古である。 == 氏子地域 == ===小倉北区=== * [[室町 (北九州市)|室町]]1－3丁目 * [[魚町 (北九州市)|魚町]] * [[京町 (北九州市)|京町]] * [[堺町 (北九州市)|堺町]] * [[鍛冶町 (北九州市)|鍛冶町]] * 米町 * 船頭町 * [[紺屋町 (北九州市)|紺屋町]] * 船場町 * [[浅野 (北九州市)|浅野]] * 長浜 * 末広 * [[藍島]] 蒲生八幡宮は、1570年代に兵火に罹り社殿が焼失する。その後、蒲生から鎮座の地を遷し、高浜（現在の[[井筒屋]]付近）に再造立され、小倉城主・城下周辺の[[氏神]]となる。以後、現在も小倉中心街、長浜、末広、藍島の[[氏神神社]]は'''蒲生八幡宮'''となる。 [[境内]]には[[1923年]]（大正12年）に'''京町・宮地嶽神社'''（現在[[コレット (百貨店)|コレット]]屋上に鎮座。瘡守稲荷）の[[氏子]]から[[奉納]]された昇格記念碑や、[[江戸]]・[[明治]]・[[大正]]・[[昭和]]時代に八百屋町（室町）・寶町　（京町船場町）・京町・[[魚町 (北九州市)|魚町]]・[[鍛冶町 (北九州市)|鍛冶町]]・長浜・船場町・舟町（浅野）など、小倉北区氏子から奉納された[[燈籠]]・[[狛犬]]等が数多く残る。　 蒲生八幡宮が管掌する神社のなかに、'''瑜伽神社'''（小倉北区船場町鎮座 小倉[[発祥]]の地）、'''貴布祢神社'''（小倉北区長浜鎮座 [[細川忠興]]から[[小笠原家]]に譲られ、400年以上の歴史をもつ[[神輿]]が残る）、'''祇園社'''（小倉南区長尾鎮座 小倉地区祇園祭発祥の社であり、小倉城下[[八坂神社 (北九州市)|八坂神社]]の本宮）など小倉の歴史において重要な神社が残る。 ===小倉南区=== *[[蒲生 (北九州市)|蒲生]] *南方 *高野 *[[徳力 (北九州市)|徳力]] *守恒 *石田 *企救丘 *山手 *星和台 *日の出町 *南丘（旧・蒲生村内、現・小倉北区） *長尾1・2丁目（旧・祇園町） *葉山町 *[[北方 (北九州市)|北方]] == 交通 == * [[西鉄バス北九州]]南方バス停下車、徒歩約6分 == 脚注 == {{DEFAULTSORT:かもうはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|50|13.85|N|130|51|30.5|E|region:JP-40_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Empress Jingū
Munakata Sanjoshin
Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto
Hosokawa Yūsai
Hosokawa Tadaoki |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north. Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|ja|三種の神器|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan|lt=Three Sacred Treasures}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|ja|小倉城}} in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusuna Shrine|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Kitakyushu]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Hosokawa Yūsai
Hosokawa Tadaoki |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=draft}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft}} == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|ja|三種の神器|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=draft}}. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|ja|小倉城|12=draft}} in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusuna Shrine|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=draft}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Kitakyushu]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]]

During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|ja|小倉城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusuna Shrine|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Kitakyushu]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]]

His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|ja|小倉城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1602 (Keichō 7). The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north. Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Kitakyushu]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Kitakyushu]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north. Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture]]

Hosokawa Tadaoki |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north. Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:小倉南区の建築物]] [[Category:小倉南区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture]]

In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:History of Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Hosokawa Tadaoki |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] == History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north. Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine. During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7). In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011. == Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province. == Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima === Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line. == Notes == {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:History of Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.

'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=|1=Kamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima

'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|qq=|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|qq=|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

<!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--><!--Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Kitakyushu)--> [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Buildings in Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:History of Kokura-Minami Ward]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11621338}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]]
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the [[:en:Imperial Regalia of Japan|Three Sacred Treasures]] after the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]], praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] during the construction of [[:en:Kokura Castle|Kokura Castle]] in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />
