revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621338]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki}}
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|WD=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|en|Kokura Castle|WD=Q581644|lt=Kokura Castle}} in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />
{{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)|version=103858456|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (北九州市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki}}
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|WD=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|en|Kokura Castle|WD=Q581644|lt=Kokura Castle}} in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1207509}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki|1=Hosokawa Tadaoki}}
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|WD=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki|1=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|en|Kokura Castle|WD=Q581644|lt=Kokura Castle|1=Kokura Castle}} in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=Apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:IMG kamou shrine.jpg |location = Kamō, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83719444444444|N|130.85847222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Kitakyushu|qid=Q188806|1=Kitakyushu}} }}
'''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamō, Kitakyushu|ja|蒲生 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō, Kitakyushu|1=Gamō, Kitakyushu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621331}}, Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine is designated as a former prefectural shrine.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja|細川幽斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Fujitaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 細川藤孝|1=Hosokawa Fujitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1207509}} * {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|qid=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki|1=Hosokawa Tadaoki}}
== History == According to legend, the Munakata Sanjoshin (Tagirihime no Mikoto, Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, and Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) descended upon the Rainbow Mountain (巣山) in Kamō village, declaring, "We will protect this land forever." Shrines were then built to honor them near the Purple Pond (紫の池), at the mountain summit, and in the north.
Later, Emperor Ōjin and Empress Jingū were enshrined alongside the Sanjoshin, establishing the shrine as Kamō Hachimangū. During the Genryaku era (1184–1185), local fisherman Iwamatsu Yozō recovered the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}} after the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|qid=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}}, praying at the shrine for success. His efforts led to recognition by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, who granted him the district, which Iwamatsu then donated to the shrine.
During the Sengoku period, the shrine was burned down during the conflicts involving {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1185032}}. A relocated shrine was built in Takahama (now near {{ill|Izutsuya|ja|井筒屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izutsuya|1=Izutsuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11373747}}), renamed Kiku (Kikuyama) Hachimangū. It was later restored to its original location and name under {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|qid=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki|1=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} during the construction of {{ill|Kokura Castle|en|Kokura Castle|qid=Q581644|lt=Kokura Castle|1=Kokura Castle}} in 1602 (Keichō 7).
In 2011, the shrine's buildings were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the Kitakyushu Board of Education.<ref>[http://www.nishinippon.co.jp/nnp/item/279760 蒲生八幡神社を市有形文化財に指定] 西日本新聞, December 28, 2011.</ref>
== Features == * Oldest shrine in Kokura, Kitakyushu. * Traditionally served as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} for the region. * Known for its expansive parish, covering much of central Kokura and beyond. * Revered as a site for {{ill|Apotropaic magic|ja|厄除|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apotropaic magic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=Apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q620591}} (disaster prevention and warding off evil). * The honden is the oldest shrine building in the former Buzen Province.
== Parish Regions == === Kokurakita-ku === * Muromachi * Uomachi * Kyōmachi * Sakai-machi * Kaji-machi * Yonemachi * Sendō-machi * Konya-machi * Semba-machi * Asano * Nagahama * Suehiro * Ainoshima
=== Kokuraminami-ku === * Kamō * Minamikata * Kōno * Tokuriki * Moritsune * Ishida * Kikugaoka * Yamate * Seiwadai * Hinode-chō * Minamioka * Nagao * Hayama-chō * Kitakata
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes' walk from Nankata Bus Stop on the Nishitetsu Bus Kitakyushu line.
== Notes == <references />
